fly-config.scenario: How many machines should we run on fly.io, and do we need BEAM clustering for LiveView state?
fly-config.decision: Single machine on fly.io (sjc region); auto-stop/auto-start; no clustering needed; all GenServer state on one node

state-persistence.scenario: Should decision state be persisted to a database, ETS, or live only in GenServer memory?
state-persistence.decision: Ephemeral GenServers: all decision state in memory; accept state loss on restart for prototype scope

components.scenario: How should we structure UI components across the decision stages - reusable LiveComponents, inline function components, or duplication?
components.decision: Inline function components (attr + slot, no stateful LiveComponent) for shared layout; per-stage inline heex for unique mechanics

css.scenario: Which CSS approach should we use for styling the Phoenix LiveView app?
css.decision: Tailwind CSS + DaisyUI: Phoenix ships Tailwind by default; DaisyUI adds component classes (card, btn, badge, input) with custom dark/light themes in oklch

blind-mechanic.scenario: When multiple participants submit priorities or options, when should each participant's input become visible to the others?
blind-mechanic.decision: All inputs visible live as you type; confirm button separates "I'm done" from "I'm ready to advance"

claude-suggestions.scenario: How should Claude's suggested priorities and options be presented and adopted by participants?
claude-suggestions.decision: Toggle model: anyone can include/exclude a suggestion; last write wins; broadcast immediately

scenario-consensus.scenario: How do participants agree on the final scenario framing before advancing to priorities?
scenario-consensus.decision: Unanimous vote: all connected participants must select the same candidate to advance

layout.scenario: How do we position decision circles on the lobby canvas and participant cards on stage views so that layouts are non-overlapping, visually balanced, responsive to viewport size, and work within LiveVi
layout.decision: Three-layer architecture: (1) CSS fixed shell for persistent navigation, (2) D3 force simulation in JS hooks for position computation, (3) scale-to-fit transform on a virtual canvas for viewport adaptation. Two distinct D3 hooks serve the lobby canvas and stage views respectively.

prompt-strategy.scenario: Should the scaffolding LLM call (generating for/against points per option) be one call for all options or one call per option?
prompt-strategy.decision: Single call: all options + priorities in one request; output array of {name, for, against} per option

response-format.scenario: How do we ensure LLM responses are machine-parseable JSON that maps predictably to our Elixir structs?
response-format.decision: Prompt coercion: system prompt instructs model to return only raw JSON matching the specified schema; no tool use

invites.scenario: How do invited participants discover and navigate to a decision they've been invited to join?
invites.decision: Per-user PubSub topic (`"user:#{username}"`) delivers invite notifications; canvas shows a modal; creator uses add/remove list UI with UserRegistry autocomplete

user-registry.scenario: How does the invite UI know which usernames exist in the system so the creator can autocomplete when inviting participants?
user-registry.decision: ETS-backed GenServer that registers every username on /join; `list_usernames/0` feeds an HTML `<datalist>` for autocomplete

pubsub.scenario: How does the Decision.Server broadcast state changes to all connected LiveViews for a given decision?
pubsub.decision: Broadcast full Decision struct on every state change; LiveViews assign and re-render

session-identity.scenario: How do we identify participants across LiveView connections without building an authentication system?
session-identity.decision: Username stored in Plug session via POST /session; session cookie persists the identity; no database, no accounts, no passwords

spectator-view.scenario: How do non-participants (observers, dropped users) view a decision in progress without disrupting it?
spectator-view.decision: Same decision_live.ex; check `username in decision.connected` to gate input controls; spectators see all but can't interact

core-architecture.scenario: How do we structure the decision state machine so it is testable, predictable, and easy to reason about under concurrent LiveView connections?
core-architecture.decision: Pure Core + GenServer Shell: Core.handle/2 returns {:ok, decision, [effects]}, Server GenServer executes effects

debounced-calls.scenario: When multiple participants submit scenario reframings in rapid succession, should each submission trigger a separate LLM synthesis call, or should we debounce?
debounced-calls.decision: 800ms debounce: each new submission resets the timer; LLM call fires only after 800ms of quiet; managed via `{:debounce, key, delay_ms, call_spec}` effect

disconnect-grace.scenario: When a participant's LiveView disconnects (page refresh, network hiccup, tab switch on mobile), should they be immediately removed from the connected set, or should there be a grace window for reconne
disconnect-grace.decision: 5-second grace period: Server schedules a `{:disconnect_timeout, user}` message on disconnect; reconnect within 5s cancels the timer; configurable via `disconnect_grace_ms`

drop-handling.scenario: How do we handle a participant's LiveView disconnecting mid-decision without blocking the remaining participants?
drop-handling.decision: connected + participants MapSets on Decision struct; terminate/2 triggers a 5-second grace period before removal; all ready-up checks use connected; participants persists across reconnects

stage-representation.scenario: How do we represent the current stage of a decision and its stage-specific data in Elixir?
stage-representation.decision: Tagged struct per stage: %Stage.Lobby{}, %Stage.Scenario{}, etc., stored in Decision.stage

type-checking.scenario: How should we utilize Elixir's type system to improve code quality and catch bugs?
type-checking.decision: Use both Dialyzer and the gradual type system during the transition period - maximum coverage now, converge to compiler-only when gradual types mature

coverage.scenario: What level of line coverage enforcement should we adopt for our test suite, and how should we handle coverage tool limitations that make 100% unachievable without workarounds?
coverage.decision: Set threshold to 99% and accept the `defmodule` gap without workarounds.

methodology.scenario: How can we increase verification feedback and reduce the cost of testing multi-user, multi-stage decision workflows during development?
methodology.decision: Dev seed routes for instant manual stage access + LiveView integration tests for automated multi-user regression coverage. Skip browser automation to avoid brittleness.

mocks.scenario: Which mock implementation strategy should we use for isolating external dependencies (LLM calls) in tests: hand-rolled mock modules or a library like Mox?
mocks.decision: Migrate to Hammox - Mox with automatic typespec validation on mock return values via `@callback` specs
